message:
Add native share/archive E2E harness, expand HTTP suite routing, harden crypto and messaging

Why these changes were made:

1. E2E runner suite expansion (e2e_runner.rs):
   The E2E runner previously only recognized "http" as a native HTTP suite.
   Three additional HTTP-family suites (http_streamable, mcp_api_parity) and
   three share/archive suites (share, share_verify_live, archive) needed native
   runner support so they can be dispatched via `am e2e run <suite>` without
   falling through to the generic shell runner. New constants, routing logic in
   run_suite(), is_native_suite() recognition, and a dedicated
   run_native_share_archive_suite() method were added. The AM_HTTP_HARNESS_SUITE
   env var is now passed to the http harness so it knows which sub-suite to run.
   Tests were updated to assert detection of all new suite names and verify
   prefix strings are not falsely matched.

2. HTTP transport harness generalization (http_transport_harness.rs):
   Previously hardcoded to "http" suite only. Refactored to use a
   HarnessSuiteSpec pattern with suite_spec_for() dispatch, allowing the same
   harness binary to run http, http_streamable, or mcp_api_parity suites based
   on the AM_HTTP_HARNESS_SUITE env var. Artifact paths, adapter scripts, and
   error messages are now suite-aware. Focused sub-suite support is gated per
   suite since only the original http suite supports it.

3. New share/archive harness (share_archive_harness.rs):
   A new native test harness following the same managed-adapter pattern as the
   HTTP harness but targeting share, share_verify_live, and archive E2E suites.
   Controlled via AM_SHARE_ARCHIVE_HARNESS_SUITE env var. Captures deterministic
   metadata, copies legacy artifacts, writes run_summary.json, and supports both
   strict and non-strict execution modes. This is needed because these suites
   were previously only runnable via direct shell invocation, with no Rust-native
   entry point for the E2E runner to dispatch to.

4. SQL query correctness in robot.rs:
   The resolve_project_sync() and resolve_agent_id() functions used SQL LIKE
   instead of = for exact slug/name matching. This was a workaround for an
   earlier FrankenConnection limitation that has since been resolved. Using LIKE
   for exact matches is semantically incorrect (e.g., underscores in slugs would
   match any single character) and could return wrong results. Changed to = for
   correctness.

5. Manifest size guard in crypto.rs (verify_bundle):
   Before this change, verify_bundle would read the entire manifest.json into
   memory without checking its size first. A maliciously crafted bundle with an
   enormous manifest could cause OOM. Added a 10MB size check before reading,
   returning a ManifestParse error if exceeded.

6. Disable broadcast messaging (messaging.rs):
   The broadcast feature in send_message was implemented but is unsupported by
   policy. Rather than leaving complex dead code that queries all agents, filters
   by contact policy and activity, and silently produces empty recipient lists,
   the entire broadcast code path was replaced with an immediate INVALID_ARGUMENT
   error. This makes the policy explicit, removes ~30 lines of untested
   dead-code risk, and simplifies the empty-recipient error message (no longer
   needs a broadcast-specific branch).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
